figures.caption: Table 10: Ablation results for MA-PoP in the heterogeneous setting. Single-agent Best denotes the best results of single agent baseline. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three different random seeds.
Figure 3: Comparison of different number of sample times M for homogeneous LLM agents.
Table 4: Results on 2 heterogeneous LLM agents. Benchmark performances are measured in Accuracy. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three experiments.
Table 3: Results on 4 heterogeneous LLM agents. Benchmark performances are measured in Accuracy. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three experiments.

Table 2: Prediction accuracy of M = 5 agents in the heterogeneous setting across six QA benchmarks. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three different random seeds. The best result per dataset is highlighted in bold.
Table 5: Results on 5 homogeneous LLM agents with Qwen-7B. Benchmark performances are measured in Accuracy. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three experiments.
Table 6: Results on 5 homogeneous LLM agents with Llama-8B. Benchmark performances are measured in Accuracy. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three experiments.
Table 7: Results on 5 homogeneous LLM agents with Qwen-32B. Benchmark performances are measured in Accuracy. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three experiments.

Table 8: Results on 3 heterogeneous LLM agents with Qwen-32B, Falcon-34B, and Gemma-27B. Benchmark performances are measured in Accuracy. The results consist of the mean and standard deviations obtained from three experiments.
Table 11: Token usage per sample on MedMCQA with 5 heterogeneous agents. Token counts are cumulative across all rounds.
Table 9: Results on 4 different LLM agents. Performances are measured in ECE and MCE.
Figure 2: Reliability diagrams of MA-PoP with and without calibration module across four models on MedMCQA dataset.
Figure 1: Comparison among the best single model, the best SOTA MAD model, majority vote and MA-PoP across all benchmarks.
